title: Prompt Lifecycle, Monitoring, and Production Capstone · Cross-Agent Certification Board
summary: Fresh writing, coding, tool, security, and long-task cases requiring prompt design, diagnosis, cross-provider adaptation, and evaluation evidence. Planned artifact: Produce a cumulative evidence packet across unfamiliar cases, performance tasks, explanations, and root-cause repairs. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.
Combine deterministic checks, rubric review, and human inspection without treating one judge as truth.
Allocate context to governing instructions, task evidence, examples, and tool results according to value.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.prompt:
# Cross-agent prompt portfolio
# Add tested contracts below. Keep shared intent separate from Claude and Codex adaptations.

steps.title: Plan the cumulative evidence run
steps.instruction: Order the Task Contract evidence cycle for this exam task before implementing it.
steps.why: Correct order keeps Task Contract assumptions visible before execution and verifies its result against a boundary case afterward.

content.text: Use a changed exam case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.

content.title: Ordering constraint
content.text: Order work so Task Contract has a declared contract before execution and a boundary or counterexample after it.

hints: Name the Task Contract precondition or invariant that the exam case must preserve.

Trace this concrete case before choosing: Use a changed exam case for prompt production capstone that tests this competency: Define the user, task, decision, acceptable output, and material failure modes before drafting a prompt.. Preserve inputs, constraints, an observable trace, and a boundary result.
Use the documented misconception as a counterexample target: Prompt quality can be judged without a defined task outcome.

options.text: Define the stakeholder decision, quantities, authority, and constraints for the exam Task Contract case
Choose and document the smallest defensible model or procedure for the exam Task Contract case
Execute the model while preserving inspectable intermediate evidence for the exam Task Contract case
Test boundaries and changed cases, communicate limits, and revise for the exam Task Contract case
